# Architecture

## Component Map

T-Stack is a zero-dependency, pure Markdown/YAML multi-agent development team framework for VS Code Copilot. It has seven structural zones:

### Zone 1: Agent Definitions (.github/agents/)

11 agent definition files, each a YAML-frontmatter + Markdown body .agent.md file:

| Agent | File | User-Invocable | Sub-Agents | Tools |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Orchestrator | orchestrator.agent.md | Yes | All 10 others + Ask | vscode, agent, read/readFile |
| Scout | scout.agent.md | Yes | none | vscode, execute, read, search, web |
| Architect | architect.agent.md | No | Scout | vscode, agent, read, search, web |
| Developer | developer.agent.md | No | none | vscode, read, edit, search, execute, web |
| Tester | tester.agent.md | No | none | vscode, read, edit, search |
| Senior Engineer | senior-engineer.agent.md | No | none | vscode, read, search, web |
| Security Auditor | security-auditor.agent.md | No | none | vscode, read, search, execute, web |
| Code Health | code-health.agent.md | No | none | vscode, read, edit, search, execute, web |
| DevOps | devops.agent.md | No | none | vscode, read, edit, search, execute, web |
| GitOps | gitops.agent.md | No | none | vscode, read, edit, search, execute, github/* |
| Scribe | scribe.agent.md | No | none | vscode, read, edit, search |

Invocation hierarchy:
- User -> Orchestrator -> (all sub-agents)
- User -> Scout (direct research)
- Architect -> Scout (research delegation)

### Zone 2: Skills (.github/skills/)

6 skill definitions (YAML-frontmatter + Markdown SKILL.md files):

| Skill | User-Invocable | Purpose |
|:---|:---|:---|
| setup | Yes | First-time T-Stack initialization |
| update | Yes | Post-update migration runner |
| deep-scan | No | Comprehensive project analysis |
| sprint-lifecycle | No | Phases 2-6 procedural instructions (loaded on demand) |
| worktree-management | No | Git worktree lifecycle and rules (loaded on demand) |
| blackboard-init | No | routing.md template and creation (loaded on demand) |

### Zone 3: Blackboard (.tstack/)

Shared memory substrate for agent coordination. Split into framework files (shipped, safe to overwrite) and state files (agent/user-generated, never overwritten).

Framework files: .version, README.md, team.md, sprints/README.md, migrations/*/migration.md
State files: project.md, decisions.md, routing.md, sprint-index.md, .migrated, sprints/SPRINT-*/*, docs/*
Hybrid: .github/copilot-instructions.md (framework regions + state Project Index region)
User override: team.local.md (optional, never overwritten)

### Zone 4: Distributable Template (src/)

Mirror of framework files for distribution. Contains identical copies of agents, skills, hooks, migrations, and .tstack/ framework files. State files are NEVER present in src/.

### Zone 5: CLI Package (packages/cli/)

npm package tstack-agents (v0.6.0) providing npx tstack-agents init and update. Pure Node.js (ESM), zero dependencies. Reads version.json manifest for file inventory and SHA-256 hashes.

### Zone 6: Scripts (scripts/)

Dogfood-only utilities:
- generate-version.mjs: Scans src/, computes SHA-256 hashes, writes version.json + .version
- pre-flight.mjs: SessionStart hook script (also in src/scripts/)
- sync-to-root.ps1: Copies src/ -> root (apply distributable to dogfood)
- sync-to-src.ps1: Copies root -> src/ (push dogfood to distributable)
- hooks/pre-commit: Git pre-commit hook running generate-version.mjs

### Zone 7: CI/CD (.github/workflows/)

- release.yml: Tag-triggered GitHub Release with ZIP artifact
- publish-cli.yml: Tag-triggered npm publish with provenance

## Module Boundaries

User Input -> Orchestrator -> Sub-agents (read/write blackboard)
                                  |
                                  v
                          .tstack/ Blackboard (shared state)
                                  |
                                  v
                          Sprint Directories (.tstack/sprints/SPRINT-XXX/)
                                  |
                                  v
                          Git Worktrees (.tstack/worktrees/) [optional]

## Data Flow

### 1. Installation Flow
npx tstack-agents init -> manifest.mjs reads version.json -> init.mjs copies framework+initOnly files -> configures .gitignore -> path traversal check on every destination

### 2. Update Flow
npx tstack-agents update -> manifest.mjs reads version.json -> update.mjs reads installed version -> hash comparison per file -> skip unchanged, copy changed/new -> report stats + migration instructions

### 3. Agent Workflow Data Flow
Human request -> Orchestrator reads blackboard (project.md -> decisions.md -> routing.md) -> classifies complexity -> Architect produces plan -> Human approves -> Developer implements -> Senior Engineer reviews (Medium+) -> Security Auditor + Tester review in parallel -> GitOps merges -> Scribe logs decisions

### 4. Session Start Flow
VS Code session starts -> .github/hooks/pre-flight.json triggers scripts/pre-flight.mjs -> reads version.json + .migrated -> outputs JSON { continue: true, systemMessage } -> PASS/WARN injected into agent context

### 5. Version Generation Flow (Dogfood)
git commit -> pre-commit hook -> generate-version.mjs scans src/ -> computes SHA-256 hashes -> writes version.json + .version -> auto-stages generated files

### 6. Sync Flows (Dogfood)
sync-to-root.ps1: src/ -> root (state files never synced)
sync-to-src.ps1: root -> src/ (state files never synced, leaked states deleted)
